title: GC Principal Identity Record
description: Canonical identity record for the principal who installed or authorized governance on this project. Authority anchor for all emitted governance files. Every by: field in passchangelog, registrar in ONS, principal in GEE, and Authority: line in DEV-PATH.md traces back to this record.

principal_id.description: Unique identifier — handle, GitHub username, or future DID. Must be stable across re-runs. Phase 2 MAY use a DID URI here.

role.description: owner: full authority, permanent. maintainer: audit/normalize/close-loop, no foundational changes. installer: one-time bootstrap, expires after first validated run. auditor: read-only.

name.description: Legal entity name if type is llc or corporation

jurisdiction.description: Human-readable registration jurisdiction, e.g. 'Wyoming, USA'. Phase 1 keeps this as plain text; Phase 2 can map it into DID service metadata or verifiable credential claims without changing the current field.

jurisdiction_ref.description: Optional structured jurisdiction reference for smooth Phase 2 DID/VC migration. This is not a DID Core field; it is local metadata that can later become a verifiable credential claim or DID service endpoint payload.

registry.description: Name of the corporate/entity registry, if applicable.

registration_id.description: Entity registration number or registry identifier, if applicable.

uri.description: Resolver URI for the jurisdiction or registry record, if available.

did.description: Optional Phase 2 DID URI for this legal entity or principal. DID Core-compatible identifier only; DID document resolution is out of scope for Phase 1.

succession.description: Optional backup authority if owner is unavailable

confirmed.description: Principal confirmed they have authority to install governance on this repo

statement.description: Optional: the exact attestation statement shown to the principal

installer_expiry.description: Present only when the principal's original role was installer. Records the expiry event and transition.

signing.description: Phase 1: local JOSE EdDSA signing with an Ed25519 public JWK per RFC 8037. Phase 2: W3C DID/VC can reference this key material through DID verification methods.

public_key.description: Deprecated compatibility field: base64urlUInt-encoded Ed25519 public key. Prefer public_key_jwk.x.

algorithm.description: JOSE alg value for Ed25519 signatures per RFC 8037.

public_key_jwk.description: Public JWK for verifying JWS signatures. RFC 8037 defines Ed25519 keys as kty=OKP, crv=Ed25519, x=<base64urlUInt public key>.

key_id.description: Key identifier for rotation tracking. In Phase 2 this can become or align with a DID verificationMethod id.

did_verification_method.description: Optional Phase 2 DID Core verification method URI, e.g. did:example:123#key-1.

signature.description: JWS compact serialization signing the canonical attestation fields

ci_mode.description: Present when dxp-init ran headless (no TTY). Principal is a service account or bot.

triggered_by.description: Human actor who triggered the CI run

scope.description: Scope of this principal record. Constrained to 'governance-scaffold-install' for dxp-init generated records — signals this is an identity anchor for scaffold installation, not a compliance or approval record.

does_not_certify.description: Explicit enumeration of claims this principal record does not make. Present in every dxp-init generated record to prevent overclaiming.
